label: K8s Sandbox Run
description: Apply a kubectl manifest in a fully-isolated ephemeral namespace, wait for resources to be Ready, capture pod logs / status / events, then tear down the namespace. Ideal for infrastructure dry-runs, ephemeral testing, and sandboxed workload experiments.

settings.name: operation
settings.label: Operation
settings.description: apply_and_wait: create namespace, apply manifest, wait for Ready, capture logs+events, delete namespace. apply_only: create namespace + apply, no wait/cleanup (caller is responsible for cleanup). cleanup: delete a previously-created sandbox namespace by label selector.

settings.name: manifest
settings.label: Manifest (YAML)
settings.description: Kubernetes manifest YAML to apply. Required for apply_and_wait and apply_only operations. Supports {{steps.X.yaml}} template references.
settings.placeholder:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: sandbox-probe
spec:
  containers:
    - name: probe
      image: busybox
      command: ['sh', '-c', 'echo hello && sleep 5']

settings.name: timeoutSeconds
settings.label: Timeout (seconds)
settings.description: How long to wait for resources to become Ready before declaring a timeout (apply_and_wait only).

settings.name: cpuLimit
settings.label: CPU Limit
settings.description: ResourceQuota CPU limit applied to the sandbox namespace (e.g. '2', '500m').

settings.name: memoryLimit
settings.label: Memory Limit
settings.description: ResourceQuota memory limit applied to the sandbox namespace (e.g. '4Gi', '512Mi').

settings.name: allowEgress
settings.label: Allow Egress
settings.description: When false (default), a default-deny NetworkPolicy blocks all egress from the sandbox namespace. Set to true only when the workload explicitly needs outbound network access.

settings.name: keepNamespaceOnFailure
settings.label: Keep Namespace on Failure
settings.description: When true (default), the sandbox namespace is preserved if the workload fails or times out — useful for post-mortem debugging. Set to false to always clean up.

settings.name: namespaceSelector
settings.label: Namespace Selector (cleanup)
settings.description: Label selector to identify sandbox namespaces to delete. Required for the cleanup operation. Example: 'flows-sandbox-execution=exec-abc123'.

ai.shortDescription: Ephemeral K8s sandbox: apply manifest in isolated namespace, capture results, auto-cleanup.
ai.whenToUse: Use when you need to run a workload in a fully-isolated Kubernetes namespace — for infrastructure dry-runs, ephemeral integration tests, or sandboxed experiments. Automatically enforces ResourceQuota + NetworkPolicy and cleans up when done. Prefer this over mcp_tool for bounded sandbox experiments that need lifecycle management.
ai.examplePrompt: apply this Pod manifest in a sandbox namespace, wait for it to complete, and return the logs
ai.promptHints: Reference namespace with {{steps.<id>.result.namespace}}. Reference logs with {{steps.<id>.result.logs}}. Use operation=apply_only when you want to start the workload and check back later. Use operation=cleanup to tear down a namespace from an earlier apply_only.

outputAssertions.name: sandbox_run_succeeded
outputAssertions.errorMessage: K8s sandbox run did not complete successfully — check result.status and result.errorReason for details.
